mentions.note: Stock clobbered down 4% after record $3.8B redemptions from private credit fund; honorable handling but fear narrative persists; upside capped, risk/reward unfavorable.
Showed class act when fund gates opened — employees funded redemptions with own money. Demonstrates conviction in portfolio quality; won't short or bet against.
Private equity stocks blasted off as market detects private credit crisis may be overstated; cognoscenti believe risk is overdone.
Better alternative to Blue Owl in private equity/credit space; 5% yield; superior management.
Private equity cohort rallies on confidence return and potential deal activity acceleration; employee-bought private credit exposure shows management alignment.
Private credit fund hit by redemptions; employees bought shares; fund performance should be strong; positive earnings setup.

Private credit fund redemption fears proved overblown; employees stepped in voluntarily to buy funds. Software stocks rebounded, easing Anthropic competition narrative. Stock bounced from low 100s to 133.
Cramer recently spoke to CEO David Faber; likes the situation a lot and interested in buying more—strong conviction hold.
Private equity firm; major participant in data center real estate and construction.
Private credit redemption concerns proved overblown; stock jumped 7.5% to become best S&P 500 performer, signaling strong investor confidence in PE fundamentals.
Private equity/brokerage rally justified as private credit chaos narrative proved false per Goldman Sachs redemption data (3.24% vs 5% allowed); buy over risky Blue Owl.
